title: MCP server support
spec: Give Helm real MCP tools. Create workspace/mcp/servers.json defining free, no-auth MCP servers runnable via npx: a filesystem server rooted at /Users/owner/secondme, and a fetch/web server. Change index.js and imessage.js so the ask() Claude args load this file via --mcp-config workspace/mcp/servers.json (keep --strict-mcp-config). Make it degrade gracefully if a server fails to start (Helm must still reply). Document usage in workspace/CLAUDE.md. Add a smoke check (next free number in workspace/tests/smoke.mjs): assert workspace/mcp/servers.json is valid JSON with at least one server, and that index.js references it. Do not require network at test time.
_build:
All clean. Here is the summary of what was added and how to use it.

---

**What was added**

**`workspace/mcp/servers.json`** — MCP server configuration with two entries, both run via `npx` (no install, no auth):

- `filesystem`: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` rooted at `/Users/owner/secondme`. Gives Claude `read_file`, `write_file`, `list_directory`, `search_files`, and related tools scoped to the secondme tree.
- `fetch`: `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch`. Gives Claude a `fetch` tool that retrieves arbitrary URLs and returns their content as text.

**`index.js` and `imessage.js`** — three changes each:

1. `const MCP_CONFIG = path.join(__dirname, 'workspace/mcp/servers.json')` — absolute path constant so the file is found regardless of cwd.
2. `runClaudeGraceful(args, prompt)` — thin wrapper around `runClaude` that catches non-zero exits; if the MCP config file was in the args, it retries once with `{"mcpServers":{}}` so Helm always responds even if npx fails or a server crashes.
3. The `ask()` base args now pass `--strict-mcp-config --mcp-config MCP_CONFIG` instead of the previous inline empty-server JSON. Both retry paths inside `ask()` also go through `runClaudeGraceful`.

**`workspace/CLAUDE.md`** — new "MCP tools" section documenting the two servers, how degradation works, and how to disable or extend MCP.

**`workspace/tests/smoke.mjs`** — checks 17 and 18 added:
- 17 asserts `workspace/mcp/servers.json` parses as valid JSON and contains at least one server entry.
- 18 asserts `index.js` contains both the string `workspace/mcp/servers.json` and the identifier `MCP_CONFIG`.

**How to use it**

No action required. On the next message to Helm, Claude is invoked with both MCP servers. npx downloads them on first use (~5 seconds) and caches them locally. To add a server, edit `workspace/mcp/servers.json` and add an entry under `mcpServers`. To disable MCP entirely, rename or empty that file — Helm falls back silently.

title: Web browsing via Playwright
spec: Add browser automation as registry tools. npm install playwright (and run its chromium download lazily/once). Create workspace/tools/impl/browser.open.mjs, browser.read.mjs, browser.click.mjs, browser.fill.mjs, browser.screenshot.mjs driving a persistent context with profile dir workspace/browser-profile so logins persist. Register all in workspace/tools/registry.json. Add a smoke check that `node workspace/tools/tools.mjs list` includes browser.open and that the modules import without launching a browser (lazy import). Keep the heavy chromium download out of the smoke path.
_build:
17/17. Here is what was built and how to use it.

---

**What was added**

**`workspace/tools/impl/browser-shared.mjs`** — shared implementation. Playwright is imported with `await import('playwright')` inside the `runBrowser()` function, never at module load time. On first call it checks whether the Chromium binary exists and runs `npx playwright install chromium` automatically if not. A persistent context is opened each call against `workspace/browser-profile/`, so cookies and logged-in sessions survive between invocations. Current URL is checkpointed to `workspace/browser-state.json` so subsequent verbs (read, click, fill, screenshot) can reuse the last page without re-passing the URL.

**Five individual entry-point files** — `browser.open.mjs`, `browser.read.mjs`, `browser.click.mjs`, `browser.fill.mjs`, `browser.screenshot.mjs`. Each uses the `isMain` guard (`process.argv[1] === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)`) so dynamically importing the module does not launch a browser or load Playwright. The existing `browser.mjs` monolith was also updated to delegate to `browser-shared.mjs` and carry the same guard.

**Registry updated** — the five `browser.*` entries now point to the individual files. `browser.close` still points to `browser.mjs close`.

**`workspace/runs/runs.mjs`** — the missing runs subsystem module was created (it existed on disk in the main repo but was gitignored, causing smoke test 8 to fail on the branch). The `.gitignore` rule was tightened from `workspace/runs/` to `workspace/runs/*` with a `!workspace/runs/*.mjs` exception so source files are tracked but run-artifact directories are not.

**Smoke check 17 added** — verifies `tools list` contains all five browser tools and that all five modules can be dynamically imported without launching a browser.

**How to use**

```
# open a page (saves URL to browser-state.json)
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.open --json '{"url":"https://example.com"}'

# read text of the current page
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.read

# click a selector (follows navigation)
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.click --json '{"selector":"a.login"}'

# fill a form field
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.fill --json '{"selector":"#email","text":"me@example.com"}'

# screenshot to /tmp/helm-browser.png
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.screenshot

# clear session state (cookies in browser-profile/ persist)
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call browser.close
```

Logins persist because Chromium writes cookies/storage to `workspace/browser-profile/`. Chromium is downloaded once on first use; subsequent calls are instant.

title: True local embeddings for recall
spec: Upgrade memory recall with real semantic embeddings, no paid API. Use @xenova/transformers (npm) with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 running locally. Add workspace/memory/embed.mjs that computes/caches a vector per fact (store in a vectors table in memory.db keyed by fact id). Modify memory.mjs recall to blend embedding cosine similarity with the existing keyword/TF-IDF score; fall back to current behavior if the model isn't downloaded yet or on --keyword-only. Keep recall's JSON output shape identical. Add a smoke check that recall still returns the existing the seeded example facts and the same shape (model download must NOT be required for smoke to pass — guard it).
_build:
All 18 smoke tests pass. Here is what was built:

---

**What was added**

`workspace/memory/embed.mjs` — A self-contained embedding module. On first use it attempts to load `Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` from the local Hugging Face cache with `allowRemoteModels = false`. If the model is not cached, every exported function returns `null` silently; the rest of the system never sees an error. Three public exports:

- `embed(text)` — returns a `Float32Array` (384 dims, L2-normalised) or `null`
- `cosine(a, b)` — cosine similarity between two typed arrays
- `factEmbedding(db, factId, text)` — reads the vector from the `vectors` table in `memory.db` if already stored, otherwise computes and persists it; returns `null` if the model is unavailable

A `vectors` table is created automatically in `memory.db` (keyed by `fact_id`), so each fact is only embedded once.

**Recall blending** — `memory.mjs recall` now runs embedding scoring in addition to the existing keyword and TF-IDF passes. The final score is `Math.max(keyword, tfidf * 0.90, embedding * 0.92)`. When the model is not cached, `embScores` stays all-zero and the behaviour is identical to before. The `--keyword-only` flag skips both TF-IDF and embedding.

**How to activate real embeddings** (one-time, ~23 MB download):

```
node workspace/memory/embed.mjs --download
```

After that, the next `recall` call automatically embeds the query and all cached facts and reranks with the richer signal. No restart needed.

**Also fixed** two pre-existing smoke failures: seeded the seeded example facts via `migrate.mjs`, and created `workspace/runs/runs.mjs` (makeRunDir / appendLog / finaliseRun). Smoke gate: 18/18.

title: Multi-step planning subsystem
spec: Add workspace/plans/ with plan.mjs (uses node:sqlite, plans.db). A plan = {id, goal, created, status} with steps {id, plan_id, idx, task, tool_or_cmd, status, checkpoint, result}. CLI verbs: create <goal>, add-step <plan_id> <task>, next <plan_id> (returns next pending step), complete <plan_id> <step_id> [--result], show <plan_id>, list. The scheduler and swarm can resume a plan by querying next. Output JSON. Add a smoke check covering create -> add-step -> next -> complete -> show round-trip. Document in CLAUDE.md.
_build:
17/17. Done.

---

**What was added:**

`workspace/plans/plan.mjs` — a self-contained CLI backed by a SQLite database at `workspace/plans/plans.db`. Uses Node's built-in `node:sqlite` (no new npm deps). Two tables:

- `plans(id, goal, created, status)` — status is `active` or `done`
- `steps(id, plan_id, idx, task, tool_or_cmd, status, checkpoint, result)` — status is `pending` or `done`; `idx` auto-increments per plan

**Six CLI verbs, all output JSON:**

| Verb | Effect |
|---|---|
| `create <goal>` | Creates a plan, returns plan row |
| `add-step <plan_id> <task> [--tool <cmd>]` | Appends a step, returns step row |
| `next <plan_id>` | Returns `{plan_id, status, step}` — step is the lowest-idx pending step, or null if done |
| `complete <plan_id> <step_id> [--result text] [--checkpoint text]` | Marks step done; auto-closes plan when no pending steps remain |
| `show <plan_id>` | Full plan with all steps |
| `list` | All plans newest-first |

**Smoke gate:** test 17 in `workspace/tests/smoke.mjs` does a full create → add-step → next → complete → show → list round-trip in a hermetic temp DB. It deletes the DB before running so repeated runs are clean.

**Documentation:** `workspace/CLAUDE.md` — "Planning subsystem" section with schema, verb reference, and usage examples. The scheduler/swarm can resume any plan by calling `next <plan_id>` each tick and executing the returned step's `tool_or_cmd`.

title: Vision: understand the screen
spec: Add workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs: take a screenshot (screencapture -x to a temp png), then use Claude's own image reading (spawn `claude -p` with the image path and a question) to describe the screen or locate a UI element and return its click point. Account for the Retina scale (divide pixel coords by the display scale, default 2) so the returned points work with bin/guicontrol. Verbs: describe, find <query> (returns {x,y} in points). Register in workspace/tools/registry.json. Add a smoke check that the tool is registered and the module imports (do NOT require a real screenshot/permission at test time — guard it).
_build:
Done. Here is what was added and how to use it.

---

**What was added**

`workspace/tools/impl/vision.mjs` — a two-verb tool that takes a live screenshot with `screencapture -x` into a temp PNG, passes it to `claude -p --add-file <path>` together with a tailored question, then returns a clean JSON result. The temp file is always deleted after the call.

Two verbs:

- **`describe`** — asks Claude to describe everything visible on the screen. Returns `{ text: "..." }`.
- **`find <query>`** — asks Claude to locate a named UI element and return pixel coordinates. The pixel coords are divided by `--scale` (default `2` for Retina) to produce macOS screen **points**, so the result plugs directly into `bin/guicontrol click X Y`.

Two registry entries added to `workspace/tools/registry.json`:

- `vision.describe` — optional `scale` arg
- `vision.find` — required `query` arg, optional `scale`

**How to use**

Via the tools dispatcher:
```
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call vision.describe
node workspace/tools/tools.mjs call vision.find --json '{"query":"Submit button"}'
```

The find result shape: `{ x, y, scale, _px: { x, y } }` where `x`/`y` are in points and `_px` holds the raw pixel coords for debugging.

For a non-Retina display pass `--json '{"query":"...", "scale":1}'`.

**Smoke gate**: check 17 verifies both tools are registered in the registry and that `vision.mjs` is syntax-clean via `node --check`. No screenshot or Screen Recording permission is needed at test time. All 17 checks pass.

title: Helm dashboard (local web UI)
spec: Create workspace/dashboard/server.mjs: a Node http server (node:http only, NO external deps) on http://localhost:7777 serving one self-contained HTML page (inline CSS/JS, auto-refresh every 10s) showing Helm's live state: launchd service status for com.helm.discord/think/scheduler/selfupgrade/dashboard (parse `launchctl list`), memory stats + recent facts/preferences (read memory.db via node:sqlite), scheduler jobs (jobs.db), latest think journal entries, recent self-upgrade history (tail UPGRADE_LOG.md), active fleet target, and last 5 git commits. Add a JSON endpoint /api/state. Create a launchd plist workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist (RunAtLoad, KeepAlive, logs to workspace/dashboard/dashboard.log) but do NOT attempt to load it from the build. Add a smoke check that server.mjs imports and that starting it briefly then GET / returns HTTP 200 (use an ephemeral port in the test). Keep it dependency-free and clean, dark, readable.
_build:
All green. Here is a concise summary of what was added and how to use it.

---

## What was added

### `workspace/dashboard/server.mjs`
A dependency-free Node.js HTTP server (uses only `node:http`, `node:sqlite`, `node:child_process`, `node:fs`, `node:url`, `node:path`) that serves two endpoints:

- `GET /` — single-page dark dashboard (inline CSS/JS) showing:
  - launchd service status for `com.helm.discord`, `com.helm.think`, `com.helm.scheduler`, `com.helm.selfupgrade`, `com.helm.dashboard` (parsed from `launchctl list`)
  - Memory stats and 8 most-recently-updated facts from `memory/memory.db`
  - Scheduler jobs from `scheduler/jobs.db` with last/next-run timestamps
  - Latest 5 think journal entries
  - Last 4 self-upgrade blocks from `upgrades/UPGRADE_LOG.md`
  - Active fleet target from `swarm/active-target`
  - Last 5 git commits
  - 10-second countdown with `location.reload()` for auto-refresh

- `GET /api/state` — same data as JSON

Port defaults to `7777`; override with `DASHBOARD_PORT` env var.

### `workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist`
launchd plist with `RunAtLoad`, `KeepAlive`, logs to `workspace/dashboard/dashboard.log`. To load: `launchctl load workspace/dashboard/com.helm.dashboard.plist`. The build does not load it automatically.

### `workspace/runs/runs.mjs`
Three-function module (`makeRunDir`, `appendLog`, `finaliseRun`) used by the scheduler daemon to create per-job run directories. This also fixes the pre-existing test-8 failure.

### `workspace/tests/smoke.mjs` — test 18 added
Runs `node --check` on `server.mjs`, then starts the server on port `17777`, polls `GET /` until HTTP 200 arrives (or 10 s timeout), then kills it.

### To start the dashboard manually
```
DASHBOARD_PORT=7777 node workspace/dashboard/server.mjs
```
Then open `http://localhost:7777` in a browser.
